access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q28.explanation: Đoạn B tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "The branch of knowledge which deals with moral principles is known as ethics. Ethical concerns about autonomous cars are explored within an interdisciplinary field called "robot ethics" which brings together experts in engineering, computing, law, philosophy, sociology and psychology as robot ethicists seek to understand the implications of autonomous robots in human society.". Heading x (A multi-faceted domain) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q28.evidence: The branch of knowledge which deals with moral principles is known as ethics. Ethical concerns about autonomous cars are explored within an interdisciplinary field called "robot ethics" which brings together experts in engineering, computing, law, philosophy, sociology and psychology as robot ethicists seek to understand the implications of autonomous robots in human society._

q29.explanation: Đoạn C tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "The potential autonomous cars have to significantly increase road safety is not disputed. According to Bryant Walker Smith from the University of South Carolina, 90% of all traffic accidents are caused by human error at least in part, and removing the error component would dramatically reduce the number of those accidents.". Heading vi (The key benefit of autonomous cars) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q29.evidence: The potential autonomous cars have to significantly increase road safety is not disputed. According to Bryant Walker Smith from the University of South Carolina, 90% of all traffic accidents are caused by human error at least in part, and removing the error component would dramatically reduce the number of those accidents._

q30.explanation: Đoạn D tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "However, in order to eliminate the effect of human error, all vehicles would need to be fully autonomous. Yet it seems unlikely the public would accept a situation in which we relinquish all manual control of vehicles.". Heading ii (The culpability conundrum) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q30.evidence: However, in order to eliminate the effect of human error, all vehicles would need to be fully autonomous. Yet it seems unlikely the public would accept a situation in which we relinquish all manual control of vehicles._

q31.explanation: Đoạn A tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "The ethics of autonomous vehicles – those that effectively drive themselves without human intervention – has become a growing concern recently as such cars start to appear on public roads. This has provoked an urgent need to address the contentious issues surrounding this developing technology before it becomes widespread. However, the issues raised are not easy to resolve, highlighting the frequent inadequacy of our own moral judgements and perhaps asking us to take responsibility for the pre-programmed morals of a machine.". Heading viii (An initial solution) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q31.evidence: The ethics of autonomous vehicles – those that effectively drive themselves without human intervention – has become a growing concern recently as such cars start to appear on public roads. This has provoked an urgent need to address the contentious issues surrounding this developing technology before it becomes widespread. However, the issues raised are not easy to resolve, highlighting the frequent inadequacy of our own moral judgements and perhaps asking us to take responsibility for the pre-programmed morals of a machine._

q32.explanation: Đoạn F tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "Robot ethicists were quick to point to some flaws in this draft proposal. While it is easy to argue that a car should crash into things not people, what should it do if it has to choose between crashing into one group of people over another?". Heading vii (The accident dilemma) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q32.evidence: Robot ethicists were quick to point to some flaws in this draft proposal. While it is easy to argue that a car should crash into things not people, what should it do if it has to choose between crashing into one group of people over another?_

q33.explanation: Đoạn G tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "Bryan Casey of Stanford University considers the Trolley Problem irrelevant for autonomous vehicles, and holds that the problem is not even ethical, but simply legal. He believes all points of contention will ultimately be tested and resolved by the courts, and argues that the rules programmed into cars will be designed not to adhere to an impossible and slippery sense of innate morality, but to keep the car within the law.". Heading i (The likely solution) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q33.evidence: Bryan Casey of Stanford University considers the Trolley Problem irrelevant for autonomous vehicles, and holds that the problem is not even ethical, but simply legal. He believes all points of contention will ultimately be tested and resolved by the courts, and argues that the rules programmed into cars will be designed not to adhere to an impossible and slippery sense of innate morality, but to keep the car within the law._
